questions.question: What is the 'open' function used for in file I/O?
options: A) To create a new file
B) To read from a file
C) To write to a file
D) To close a file
questions.explanation: The 'open' function is used to open an existing file for reading or writing.

questions.question: What is the 'fcntl' function used for?
options: A) To read from a file
B) To write to a file
C) To close a file
D) To control file descriptors
questions.explanation: The 'fcntl' function is used to control file descriptors and implement various file I/O operations.

questions.question: What is the 'create' function used for in file I/O?
options: A) To open an existing file
B) To create a new file
C) To read from a file
D) To write to a file
questions.explanation: The 'create' function is used to create a new file for reading or writing.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'fcntl' function in the 'open' system call?
options: A) To set file permissions
B) To set file access modes
C) To implement file I/O operations
D) To close file descriptors
questions.explanation: The 'fcntl' function is used to implement file I/O operations such as locking, setting file permissions, and more.

questions.question: What is the 'fcntl' function used to set in the 'open' system call?
options: A) Read-only access
B) Write-only access
C) Read-write access and file locking
D) File sharing and synchronization
questions.explanation: The 'fcntl' function is used to set read-write access and implement file locking in the 'open' system call.
